# FSVolumeKernelOffloadedIOOperations

**Protocol**

Methods and properties implemented by volumes that use kernel-offloaded I/O to achieve higher file transfer performance.

## Declaration

```swift
protocol FSVolumeKernelOffloadedIOOperations : NSObjectProtocol
```

## Overview

A volume that conforms to this protocol supplies file extent mappings to FSKit, which allows file transfers to take place in the kernel. This approach provides higher-performance file transfer than transferring data between the module and kernel, while still allowing the file system to run in user space.

This protocol uses *extents* to provide the kernel the logical-to-physical mapping of a given file. An extent describes a physical offset on disk, and a length and a logical offset within the file. You don’t manage extents directly. Instead, FSKit provides you with an [doc://FSKit/documentation/FSKit/FSExtentPacker] to define and pack the extents in your implementations of this protocol’s methods.

Most volumes conform to either this protocol or [doc://FSKit/documentation/FSKit/FSVolume/ReadWriteOperations]. You can conform to both if you need to provide kernel-offloaded I/O only for certain files. In that case, files with the [doc://FSKit/documentation/FSKit/FSItem/Attribute/inhibitKernelOffloadedIO] attribute set use [doc://FSKit/documentation/FSKit/FSVolume/ReadWriteOperations], and those without it use this protocol. A volume that doesn’t conform to either protocol can’t support any I/O operation.

## Performing mapped I/O

- **blockmapFile(_:offset:length:flags:operationID:packer:replyHandler:)**: Maps a file’s disk space into extents, allowing the kernel to perform I/O with that space.
- **FSBlockmapFlags**: Flags that describe the behavior of a blockmap operation.
- **completeIO(for:offset:length:status:flags:operationID:replyHandler:)**: Completes an I/O operation for a given file.
- **FSCompleteIOFlags**: Flags that describe the behavior of an I/O completion operation.

## Working with items

- **createFile(name:in:attributes:packer:replyHandler:)**: Creates a new file item and map its disk space.
- **FSItem.SetAttributesRequest**: A request to set attributes on an item.
- **lookupItem(name:in:packer:replyHandler:)**: Looks up an item within a directory and maps its disk space.
- **preallocateSpace(for:at:length:flags:packer:replyHandler:)**: Preallocates and maps disk space for the given file.
- **FSVolume.PreallocateFlags**: Behavior flags for preallocation operations.

## Mapping file extents

- **FSExtentPacker**: A type that directs the kernel to map space on disk to a specific file managed by this file system.

## Implementing optional operations

- **FSVolume.OpenCloseOperations**: Methods and properties implemented by volumes that want to receive open and close calls for each item.
- **FSVolume.ReadWriteOperations**: Methods implemented for read and write operations that deliver data to and from the extension.
- **FSVolume.AccessCheckOperations**: Methods and properties implemented by volumes that want to enforce access check operations.
- **FSVolume.RenameOperations**: Methods and properties implemented by volumes that support renaming the volume.
- **FSVolume.PreallocateOperations**: Methods and properties implemented by volumes that want to offer preallocation functions.
- **FSVolume.XattrOperations**: Methods and properties implemented by volumes that natively or partially support extended attributes.
- **FSVolume.ItemDeactivation**: Methods and properties implemented by volumes that support deactivating items.

## Inherits From

- NSObjectProtocol
